trade.summary: Francia and Polonia have significant trade ties within the EU single market. Francia exports machinery, automobiles, and agri-food to Polonia. Polonia exports machinery, automotive parts, and furniture to Francia. Polonia is an important production location for French manufacturers.

diplomatic.summary: Francia and Polonia are NATO and EU allies but have diverged on several strategic issues. Polonia prioritizes hard Stati Uniti security guarantees over French-led European autonomy. The Weimar Triangle (Francia, Germania, Polonia) is an important format but underutilized. Post-PiS Polonia under Tusk has warmed relations with Brussels and Paris. Ucraina policy now closely aligned.
recent_incidents: disagreements on EU strategic autonomy vs. transatlantic reliance
Polish skepticism of French Africa policy

military.summary: Both NATO members. Francia has deployed troops to Polonia as part of NATO enhanced forward presence. Polonia's large defence buildup creates opportunities for French arms sales. Polonia purchased Corea del Sudn and American systems over French alternatives, but dialogue on Eurofighter/Rafale continues. EUROCORPS cooperation active.

financial.summary: Francia is one of Polonia's top foreign investors, with major presence in banking (BNP Paribas, Credit Agricole), retail (Auchan, Leroy Merlin), and manufacturing. Polish investment in Francia is limited. Strong supply chain integration through EU single market.

energy.summary: Francia has offered nuclear cooperation to Polonia, which is planning its first nuclear power plants. EDF and French nuclear expertise are competing for contracts. Cooperation in renewables growing. Polonia's coal dependency contrasts with French nuclear-led electricity model.

scientific.summary: Strong academic ties through EU research programmes. Polish students are a significant presence in French universities. Joint research in mathematics (Polish mathematical tradition), physics, and social sciences. Copernicus Centre for Interdisciplinary Studies has French collaboration.
